reasoning: The Los Angeles Clippers currently sit around the 6th seed in the highly competitive Western Conference. While they retain star power in Kawhi Leonard (who recently returned from injury) and James Harden, and they upgraded their roster at the trade deadline (putting them in the Top 10 of recent power rankings), the Western Conference is historically stacked this season. Unprecedented 2025 trade deadline moves saw Luka Doncic join LeBron James on the Lakers, and Jimmy Butler join Steph Curry on the Warriors. With the Thunder, Nuggets, Timberwolves, Lakers, and Warriors all presenting massive playoff hurdles, the Clippers face a brutal path to the NBA Finals. The Polymarket price in mid-February hovered around 6%, which accurately reflects the Clippers' status as a formidable but secondary contender in an incredibly top-heavy conference. A 6% probability balances their experienced roster and recent improvement against the juggernauts they will inevitably face in the playoffs.
